plainEnglish: Pre-recorded audio-only media (like a podcast) needs a text transcript that carries the same information. Pre-recorded video with no audio (like a silent screen recording) needs either a transcript or an audio track describing what happens. The point is that people who can't hear the audio, or can't see the video, can still get the content another way.
whyItMatters: Deaf users can't access a podcast that has no transcript, and blind users can't follow a silent how-to video that has no audio description or text alternative.
quickCheck: Confirm every <audio> podcast links to a transcript and every silent <video> has a transcript or descriptive audio track—there's no automated tool for this, so verify it manually.
commonMistakes: Publishing a podcast or recorded interview with no transcript at all.
Assuming auto-generated captions count as a transcript for audio-only content—a transcript must be a separate, readable document.
Posting a silent product demo without any text or audio describing what's shown on screen.
Linking a transcript that omits speaker names or non-speech audio that carries meaning.

codeExamples.label: Audio with a linked transcript
